name: TAO 4h Inventory Reset Reclaim v1
edge_mechanism: price flushes through short-term value, weak inventory is cleared, value is reclaimed, and continuation resumes from a cleaner location than a static oversold bounce
thesis: Cycle 78 explicitly asks for inventory-reset continuation after flush-and-reclaim rather than static oversold bounce logic. TAO 4h is the best-fit lane because the trend-up regime gives reclaim continuation a real structural reason to exist. The edge is the reclaim after the flush, not trying to catch the bottom tick.
invalidation_condition: If TAO 4h flush-and-reclaim events remain too sparse or repeatedly fail back under value, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price flushes below short-term value, weak inventory is cleared, value is reclaimed, and continuation resumes in the dominant direction.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Trend-capable assets often need a final inventory washout before continuation can resume. Requiring reclaim after the flush should improve location versus a static oversold bounce.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best fit because it is the live trend-up primary lane and has enough amplitude for reset-reclaim continuation to matter.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The reclaim may only be a weak bounce within unresolved chop, causing continuation to fail quickly.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If first targets hit but runners fail, de-risk faster after entry before touching the reclaim trigger.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Several reclaimed reset entries, many modest first-target wins, and fewer larger continuation runners.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h clears density and reset-reclaim continuation outperforms the prior delayed-retest family.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-12 trades, or repeated reclaim entries that fail back under value immediately.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because flush-and-reclaim is simpler and denser than delayed retest logic

test_lanes.why_included: Best-fit trend-up lane for inventory-reset reclaim continuation.
test_lanes.continue_if: TAO 4h shows enough reset-reclaim continuations with acceptable first-target behavior.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h reclaim events remain too sparse or fail immediately.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Moderate participation with repeated reset continuations.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same reclaim grammar survives on a flatter similar lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH 4h retains a smaller but still tradable reset-reclaim behavior.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms the thesis needs stronger trend contamination.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Smaller and more balance-like reclaim continuations than TAO.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether the same reset-reclaim grammar survives a weaker and less stable cross-lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: AXS 4h shows enough reclaim continuations to prove portability.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: AXS 4h turns the idea into weak bounce noise.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Lower count and more fragile reclaim continuations than TAO.

long: Exit remaining 30% on loss of VWAP or 6 bars
short: Exit remaining 30% on reclaim of VWAP or 6 bars
